question: Pes Planus Distortion
answer: Pes planus (collasped arch) & knee valgus & adducted internal rotate hip (pigeon toe)

question: Pes Planus Distortion overactive
answer: Gastro and soleus (calves) & Adductor complex (inner thigh) & Hip flexor)

question: Pes Planus Distortion underactive
answer: Anterior and posterior tibialis (shin) & Gluteus maximus and medius (booty)

question: Lower crossed syndrome
answer: Anterior pevlic tilt & excessive lordosis (lumbar extension)

question: Lower crossed syndrome overactive
answer: Hip flexor & lumbar extensors

question: Lower crossed syndrome underactive
answer: Gluteus max and medius & hamstring complex & abdominals

question: Upper crossed syndrome
answer: Thoracic spine (hunch) & protracted and inernally rotated shoulders & head and neck jutted forward

question: Upper crossed syndrome overactive
answer: Pectoralis major & levator scapula and sternocldeidomoid (neck) & Upper trapezius

question: Upper crossed syndrome underactive
answer: Middle and lower trapezius and rhomboids (mid back) & Deep cervical flexors (muscle deep within neck)

question: Overhead squat: ankles out overactive
answer: Gastrocnemius/soleus (calves) & Hamstrings complex

question: Overhead squat: ankles out underactiveactive
answer: Anterior and posterior tibialis (shin muscles) & Gluteus maximus and medius

question: Overhead squat: knee valgus overactive
answer: Tensor fascia latae (TFL)(muscle near front of hip) & Adductor complex (inner thigh muscles)

question: Overhead squat: knee valgus underactive
answer: Gluteus maximus and medius & Anterior and posterior tibialis

question: Overhead squat: low back arch overactive
answer: Hip flexors (rectus femoris, psoas, TFL) & Lumbar extensors (low-back muscles) & Latissimus dorsi (large back muscle)

question: Overhead squat: low back arch underactive
answer: Gluteus maximus & Hamstrings complex & Abdominals

question: Overhead squat: forward lean arch overactive
answer: Hip flexors & Gastrocnemius/soleus & Rectus abdominis and external obliques (superficial abdominal muscles)

question: Overhead squat: forward lean arch underactive
answer: Gluteus maximus & Hamstrings complex & Lumbar extensors

question: Overhead squat: arms forward arch overactive
answer: Latissimus dorsi & Pectoralis major and minor (chest muscles) & Teres major (posterior shoulder muscle)

question: Overhead squat: arms forward arch underactive
answer: Middle and lower trapezius (mid-back muscle) & Rhomboids (muscles near shoulder blades) & Posterior deltoids (back of shoulder muscles) & Portions of the rotator cuff (small muscles that stabilize the shoulder)

question: Single leg squat valgus overactive
answer: Tensor fascia latae (TFL) & Adductor complex

question: Single leg squat valgus underactive
answer: Gluteus maximus and medius & Anterior and posterior tibialis

question: Push assessment low back arch overactive
answer: Hip flexors (rectus femoris, psoas, TFL) & Lumbar extensors

question: Push assessment low back arch underactive
answer: Gluteus maximus & Hamstrings complex & Abdominals

question: Push assessment Scapular elevation arch overactive
answer: Levator scapulae (posterior neck muscles) & Upper trapezius

question: Push assessment Scapular elevation arch underactive
answer: Lower trapezius

question: Push assessment Head juts forward overactive
answer: Levator scapulae & Sternocleidomastoid (anterior neck muscles)

question: Push assessment Head juts forward underactive
answer: Deep cervical flexors (deep neck stabilizer muscles)

question: Pull assessment low back arch overactive
answer: Hip flexors (rectus femoris, psoas, TFL) & Lumbar extensors

question: Pull assessment low back arch underactive
answer: Gluteus maximus & Hamstrings complex & Abdominals

question: Pull assessment Scapular elevation arch overactive
answer: Levator scapulae (posterior neck muscles) & Upper trapezius

question: Pull assessment Scapular elevation arch underactive
answer: Lower trapezius

question: Pull assessment Head juts forward overactive
answer: Levator scapulae & Sternocleidomastoid (anterior neck muscles)

question: Pull assessment Head juts forward underactive
answer: Deep cervical flexors (deep neck stabilizer muscles)
